tweets.text:
.@nicksdjohnson says his belief that token governance can work is 'weaker than it was.' 🗳️

On Uneasy Money, he tells @kaiynne and @tayvano_ why he still isn't ready to give up on the ENS DAO.

Timestamps:
🗳️ 02:14 Kain unpacks whether Nick really controls the ENS Security Council vote
🔥 06:37 Taylor pushes back: is the pile-on on Nick actually fair?
⚔️ 11:30 Kain calls it a DAO governance proxy war, echoing Aave's Stani fight
🍯 15:00 Nick on why a DAO treasury becomes a honeypot for capital allocation
🪙 28:01 Nick on why ETH-weighted voting would have left ENS open to a takeover
⚖️ 32:47 Nick clarifies what the ENS Labs proposal actually changes at the DAO
🏛️ 40:27 Nick argues the DAO should stop trying to run ENS day-to-day
📣 52:04 Cape: Get 33% off your first six months with code unchained at https://t.co/2ZtLziXsHV
🍴 58:56 Could a fork let Nick walk off with ENS's treasury? Alex says no
🎤 01:13:31 Taylor asks Nick and Alex point blank: why are you still here?

tweets.text:
I start with 100 rare oranges that never spoil. They are so rare these are the only 100 that will ever exist. 

I give 30 away and sell 20 to my friends. Of the remaining 50, I put half in my left pocket and half in my right.

Through business activity, I earn enough money to buy 5 oranges from the marketplace where the orange community has been trading them, among other rare fruits, and put them in my shirt pocket.

My goal is to increase the value of my fruit marketplace. What is my optimal choice?

1) launch 5 oranges into the sun from my left pocket
2) launch 5 oranges into the sun from my right pocket
3) launch 5 oranges into the sun from my shirt pocket

Discuss.

tweets.text:
Robinhood launched using Arbitrum tech, joining the Arbitrum stack (Orbit).

Yet $ARB has only barely bounced back from its ATL 7 days ago.

Massive win for Arbitrum from technical, reputational, and BD perspective but also from liquidity via Orbit but market doesn't care.

Years ago this would've pumped $ARB significantly.

But market now requires fruits from this partnership, ideally increased fees that accrue to $ARB.

At least Robinhood paid $1M to Arbitrum’s Open House 2026 program.

It's also a win for Ethereum:

Instead of Robinhood building their own corpo-slop L1, Robinhood stays within the Ethereum ecosystem with some fees being routed to the L1.

So Ethereum L2 roadmap didn't go to waste despite liquidity fragmentation and worse UX in the short run.

tweets.text:
Heartbreak for Australia 💔—they frustrated Egypt and stayed in the game until the concluding penalties.
A massive, huge, enormous  congratulations to Egypt 🎉on an incredible, hard-fought victory!
Sending my sympathy to all the fans, on both sides, whose anxiety went right through the roof today.
That was an absolute rollercoaster. I was pacing the floor for the entire game! 🚶‍♂️📺
#AUSvEGY #MatchDay #WhatAGame #egypt #australia #worldcup @FIFAWorldCup @fifacom_ar

tweets.text:
How much is Myers Briggs P stuff Vs J stuff

Is it considering more lateral thinking and open mindedness exploration (Very P like) and avoiding J like thinking. deciding what's important and focusing on getting to an answer? 

My experience with these things is people over value lateral thinking and end up spinning and not deciding. But I am an INTJ os do understand I can close off lateral thinking early.

tweets.text:
There’s no fundamentals, zero sum; it’s purely speculative and driven by attention 

It’s been fun to watch the energy and excitement return to the trenches but unless holders intend to bag hold for eternity or find a greater fool I don’t get how this continues to defy gravity?

Attention will inevitably fade and this will trend to zero

so here’s where I am on the house hunt:

1: I buy a property completely DONE

new kitchen, new bathroom, new carpets, low maintenance garden, all done.

2: I buy a property that needs WORK

1: is unlikely to increase in value.

2: will increase in value.

What would you do here?

tweets.text:
nvidia’s becoming a bank.

most ai cloud providers can’t afford the $50,000-80,000 per gpu cost to build data centers so NVIDIA agrees to finance them in exchange for revenue share

it’s p surreal watching an entire economy being built around ai chips. it’s kinda akin to gold back in the day

would not surprise me to see GPU-backed currencies in the future.
